notes: Native programme subissue and Project 28 record created.

command: rg -n 'calculate_net_benefit|calculate_net_monetary_benefit_simple|net-benefit' voiage tests rust specs/software-landscape; inspect public exports, PyO3 registration, lazy runtime adapter, generated method evidence, and stable-core plan
notes: Confirmed net-benefit construction as the sole stable Python-authority gap and identified both public Python calculation paths. Recorded the existing ambiguous 2-D WTP broadcasting behavior and recommended explicit scalar, threshold-vector, and sample-by-threshold axes with a bounded compatibility path. This is pre-start evidence only: issue #316 and its Conductor track remain new pending exact scientific-freeze approval.

Added estimator-specific uncertainty, convergence, RNG, replication, stopping and numerical-error requirements together with deterministic parallel, streaming, resource-budget, analytical-oracle and metamorphic evidence.

Started the stable Rust-core track after exact scientific-contract approval and the pre-start authority audit. The first TDD task is active. Scientific approval does not waive the pending stable numerical-evidence gate or any binding, release, publication, or external gate.

command: cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml -p voiage-numerics; PYO3_PYTHON=$PWD/.venv/bin/python cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml -p voiage-python --lib; cargo bench --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml -p voiage-numerics --bench foundational --no-run; uv run --extra ci --extra dev pytest <focused-contract-tests> --no-cov; pnpm --dir docs/astro-site check; pnpm --dir docs/astro-site build; uv run --extra dev tox -e lint; uv run --extra dev tox -e vale; uv run --extra dev tox -e typecheck; uv run --extra dev tox -e docs; uv run --extra dev tox -e frontier-contract,version-sync
notes: Closed the sole stable Python-authority gap. Rust now owns scalar, threshold-vector, sample-specific threshold and explicit legacy-elementwise net-benefit arithmetic; both public Python helpers route through PyO3. Finite negative thresholds are supported, non-finite inputs/results fail closed, square sample-threshold matrices are explicitly disambiguated, and automatic v1 elementwise inference warns. All Rust numerics tests, 22 PyO3 tests, benchmark compilation, docs, lint, type, frontier, version and focused Python contract tests passed. A complete Python run found only three contract/baseline assertions caused by the new governed method and the temporary lock upgrade; all three were corrected and their focused tests passed. Mutation and final full-matrix closeout remain.
